era_context: Continuation of the Middle Bronze Age in the Levant and Egypt; the Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia; the Shang dynasty in China; the Early Bronze Age in Greece. This year falls within a period of established state systems, long-distance trade (tin from Afghanistan to Mesopotamia, cedar from the Levant to Egypt), and the development of alphabetic writing in the Levant.

events.title: Continued reign of Samsu-iluna of Babylon
events.description: Samsu-iluna continues his reign, managing the Old Babylonian state through administrative and military means. This period sees ongoing tension with peripheral territories and the gradual erosion of Babylonian power. No specific events for this particular year are well-documented in surviving sources.
events.certainty_note: Samsu-iluna's reign is well-attested, but year-by-year events are not documented. This entry represents the general political continuity of his rule.

events.title: Egyptian 13th Dynasty administration
events.description: The 13th Dynasty of Egypt continues to govern Upper Egypt during the Second Intermediate Period, maintaining administrative structures and religious practices despite the loss of centralized control over Lower Egypt (now under Hyksos influence). The dynasty is characterized by rapid succession of rulers and gradual weakening of royal authority. No specific events for this year are documented.

sources.name: Egyptian King Lists (Turin Papyrus, Abydos List)

events.certainty_note: The 13th Dynasty is poorly documented. Exact dating and succession of rulers is uncertain; this year falls within the period of the dynasty's rule but no specific events are attested.

events.title: Shang dynasty military campaigns
events.description: Oracle bone inscriptions from the Shang period record military campaigns against neighboring peoples and internal consolidation of power. The Shang kings conduct regular military expeditions to secure resources, tribute, and territorial control. These campaigns are documented through oracle bone divination records, which ask for divine approval of military actions.
events.certainty_note: Oracle bones confirm the practice of military campaigns during the Shang period. Specific campaigns for this year are not individually dated.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The general historiographic uncertainty regarding Egyptian 13th Dynasty chronology and Hyksos dating persists.
historiographic_note: 2016 BCE is poorly documented in terms of specific events. Most information comes from later compilations (king lists, chronicles) or archaeological context rather than contemporary records. The year-by-year narrative for this period is largely reconstructed from broader patterns rather than specific dated events. This reflects the general sparseness of documentation for the Middle Bronze Age outside of administrative tablets and king lists.

graph_edges.note: Samsu-iluna's reign continues from the previous year.
